name: SparkPost (Bird) API
what_it_does: SparkPost (now part of Bird) email delivery API for transactional and marketing email with advanced deliverability analytics, SMTP relay, and recipient validation.
use_cases: High-volume transactional email sending with deliverability analytics
Email address validation before sending to reduce bounce rates
A/B testing email templates with engagement tracking
Managing sending domains and IP pools for reputation management
Real-time email event webhooks for delivery and engagement tracking
not_for: Non-technical teams needing email marketing campaign tools
Simple low-volume email without deliverability requirements
best_when: You need enterprise-grade transactional email with deep deliverability analytics and ISP relationship management.
avoid_when: You need simple developer-friendly email (Resend/Postmark) or built-in marketing campaign tools.

auth.notes: API keys with granular permission scopes (send, read, manage). Passed as Authorization header. Multiple API keys with different permissions recommended for security.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 500 emails/month free; no credit card for free tier
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing with monthly tiers. SparkPost was acquired by MessageBird (Bird) — product roadmap may shift. Pricing competitive with other ESPs.

performance.rate_limits: Per-account sending limits; scalable by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include error codes and human-readable messages. Multi-recipient send errors report per-recipient failures in a results array.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Same transmission call sends duplicate emails.
known_agent_gotchas: SparkPost was acquired by Bird (MessageBird) — branding and docs may be in transition
Sending domain must have SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configured — agents should verify before first send
Dedicated IP pools require warm-up period — new IPs start with conservative sending limits
Substitution data for templates uses Handlebars-like syntax — test templates before production use
Recipient list management differs from SendGrid — understand substitution_data vs stored recipients
EU account (eu.sparkpost.com) vs US (api.sparkpost.com) have separate endpoints and data storage

security_score_components.security_notes: Transactional email API (now MessageBird/Bird). API key with permission scopes. DKIM/SPF required. Sending API can trigger billing — protect from abuse.
